claim: For all integers n > 2, Euler's totient phi(n) is even.
For all odd integers n > 2, phi(n) is divisible by 4.
For all even integers n > 2, phi(n) is divisible by 2.
For all integers n > 2, phi(n) is not congruent to 1 modulo 2.
For all odd primes p, phi(p) = p - 1 is divisible by 4.

claim: For all odd prime powers p^k with k >= 1, phi(p^k) is divisible by 4.
predicate: (n <= 1) or all(not (is_prime(p) and n % p == 0 and (n // pow(p, vp(n, p)) == 1)) or (p == 2) or (phi(n) % 4 == 0) for p in range(2, abs(n) + 1))

claim: Every odd prime p satisfies p ≡ 1 (mod 4) or p ≡ 3 (mod 4).

predicate: (n <= 2) or (len([p for p in range(3, abs(n) + 1) if is_prime(p) and n % p == 0]) < 2) or (phi(n) % 8 == 0)

predicate: (not (vp(n, 2) >= 2 and all(vp(n, p) == 0 for p in range(3, abs(n) + 1)))) or (phi(n) == pow(2, vp(n, 2) - 1))

claim: For n = 2p where p is an odd prime, phi(n) = phi(p) = p - 1 is even.

claim: For n = 2p where p is an odd prime, phi(n) = p - 1 where p is odd.

claim: The sum of phi(k) for 1 <= k <= n is always an integer, and for n > 2 is odd when n is a power of 2.
